channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thousands rally in Slovakia to mark the 2018 slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands rallied all across Slovakia on Friday to mark the seventh anniversary of the slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee. The rallies are part of a wave of protests against the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands rally in Slovakia to mark the 2018 slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee
item.description: Thousands have rallied all across Slovakia to mark the seventh anniversary of the slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis is 'fine,' condition not life-threatening, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis "is fine" as his medical condition is not life-threatening, but he is not being released from the hospital yet, this doctors in Italy announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'has no cards,' 'shouldn't be at meetings' with Russia
item.description: President Trump lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, saying he did not think it was important for Zelensky to attend negotiations about the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance criticizes Germany's free speech laws in remarks to conservatives
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance has criticized Germany’s free speech laws during an appearance at a conservative gathering outside Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sinn Féin leaders won't attend St. Patrick's event at White House to protest US stance on Gaza
item.description: Sinn Féin’s leaders say they will not attend a traditional St. Patrick’s Day event next month at the White House as a protest against President Donald Trump’s stance on Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda expresses solidarity with military general sanctioned by the US over violence in Congo
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Rwanda's government on Friday asserted solidarity with a top official sanctioned by the U.S. over violence in eastern Congo, where Rwanda-backed rebels have captured two major cities in an armed conflict that now threatens the Congolese government.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US not co-sponsoring UN resolution supporting Ukraine: Report
item.description: The U.S. is reportedly refusing to co-sponsor a draft United Nations resolution marking three years since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, signaling weakening U.S. support for Ukraine as President Trump is reaching out to Russia to end its war against the country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead — so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow doesn't attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China issued 'disconcerting' warning of live-fire exercises to planes flying above, Australia says
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Airliners were over the Tasman Sea crossing between Australia and New Zealand when the Chinese navy warned they were flying over a secret live-fire exercise, Australian Defense Minister Richard Marles said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-MCLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Facing pressure from Trump, Costa Rica and Honduras join Panama as stopovers for foreign deportees
item.description: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica (AP) — A group of families and children hailing from Uzbekistan, China, Afghanistan, Russia and more countries climbed down the stairs of an airplane in Costa Rica's capital Thursday, the first flight of deportees from other nations Costa Rica agreed to hold in detention facilit
item.dc:creator: JAVIER CÓRDOBA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French street artist Shuck One pays tribute to Black history at Pompidou Center in Paris
item.description: French street artist Shuck One is honoring Black figures who shaped France’s recent history on the mainland and overseas in an art installation.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet And Nicolas Garriga, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US aid freeze puts HIV-positive orphans in Kenya at risk as medical supplies dwindle
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Two-year-old Evans was brought to the Nyumbani Children’s Home in Nairobi, Kenya a year ago, suffering from HIV and tuberculosis. With no family to care for him, Evans was referred to the orphanage by a health center after he stopped responding to medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS KOMU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China supports 'recent consensus' between US and Russia on Ukraine war, foreign minister says
item.description: China supports “the recent consensus” between the U.S. and Russia on the war in Ukraine, said Wang Yi, the country’s foreign minister, amid ongoing discussions about bringing an end to the nearly three-year war.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Bodies released by Hamas include some of the oldest and youngest hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The bodies of some of the oldest and youngest hostages taken to Gaza at the start of the Israel-Hamas war were released Thursday, bringing the total closer to the 33 supposed to be freed under the ceasefire that started last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains of the young Bibas brothers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin removes migrants from Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers have removed deported migrants from a facility on the U.S. military base in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, where the Trump administration brought them as part of an effort to remove certain immigrants from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Remains of young mom Shiri Bibas, taken hostage and killed by Hamas, not returned despite promise, Israel says
item.description: The mother of a woman abducted with her two children and husband was not among the slain hostages returned to Israel by Hamas on Thursday, Israel said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rules Trump admin violated order to unfreeze foreign aid but declines to hold officials in contempt
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration violated a court order to temporarily unfreeze foreign aid but declined to hold officials in civil contempt over the transgression. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Sheinbaum warns US cannot 'invade' Mexico in fight against cartels
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum reacted strongly to a U.S. State Department order declaring that several of Mexico's drug cartels were terrorist organizations, which led Elon Musk to say they were “eligible for drone strikes.” “This cannot be an opportunity for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Terror attack rocks Israel; three buses reportedly explode
item.description: Israel officials have ordered all bus and train services halted while all vehicles are inspected for bombs following two bus explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guterres to propose UN assumes funding for mission struggling to fight gangs in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres said he would request that the United Nations assumes funding for the structural and logistical expenses of a multinational force in Haiti that is struggling to fight violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans in new survey say Russia started war in Ukraine
item.description: Most Americans think Russia started the war in Ukraine, with those who said Kyiv was first to attack were in single digits, according to a survey published Wednesday. The YouGov poll found that 61 percent of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Philippine village offers cash for mosquitos amid dengue outbreak
item.description: To combat a dengue outbreak, a Philippine village is offering a bounty of one Philippines peso for every five mosquitoes residents turn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yoon appears in 2 different South Korean courts while defending his martial law decree
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Shuttled around Seoul in a prison transport vehicle, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol appeared in two different courts on Thursday, contesting his arrest on rebellion charges in one and fighting an effort to remove him from office in the other.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow Mauritania’s homes and history
item.description: An ancient trading post known for housing some of West Africa's most historically significant manuscripts could soon be covered by the Sahara Desert.
item.dc:creator: Sam Metz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China, Iran and Russia condemned by dissidents at UN watchdog's Geneva summit
item.description: Dissidents from across the globe gathered in Geneva to speak about their experiences fighting oppressive regimes. Family and friends of dissidents spoke as well, some in honor of their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis got out of bed to eat breakfast on 7th day in hospital: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis, who has been hospitalized since Feb. 14, apparently got out of bed for breakfast and is showing "slight improvement" in his medical tests, according to Vatican News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia seems to have time on its side as the war in Ukraine reaches its third year
item.description: When Russian troops rolled into Ukraine three years ago, they brought their parade uniforms with them on the push to Kyiv. President Vladimir Putin expected a quick victory. What Putin dubbed the “special military operation” has turned into Europe’s largest conflict since World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China begins repatriation from Thailand of more than 1,000 online scam workers rescued from Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An airlift carrying more than 1,000 Chinese nationals who had worked at online scam centers in eastern Myanmar began Thursday, after the rescued workers were taken across the border to Thailand and put on chartered flights to China.
item.dc:creator: JUTARAT SKULPICHETRAT and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 slain Israelis, including Shiri Bibas and her two young boys
item.description: Hamas has turned over the bodies of four slain Israeli hostages more than 500 days after the terrorist group's Oct. 7, 2023, attack in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Advance of Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo threatens regional peace, UN officials warn
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Top United Nations officials in Africa warned an emergency meeting of the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday that the offensive of Rwanda-backed rebels in mineral-rich eastern Congo is threatening peace in the wider region.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two Britons detained in Iran charged with spying, Iranian judiciary says
item.description: British nationals Craig and Lindsay Foreman have been detained in Iran and charged with espionage; they allegedly collected information in different parts of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
